title: Publisher Buffers, Drain, Admission, and Bounded Batching
description: A publisher awaits a boolean from publish, ignores false, buffers unbounded work during broker flow control, and batches until memory exhaustion while upstream requests continue to receive success. Learners cumulatively produce publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Treat amqplib publish and sendToQueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or Promises.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks.
Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets.
